title: Plugin manifest
description: The declarative contract every plugin ships (system-design.md §3). The Loader reads this BEFORE any plugin code runs, so discovery, load order, version compatibility, namespacing and security are enforced from it. Cross-field rules a JSON Schema cannot express (e.g. every permission/event/provided token must be prefixed with `id` — §6) are validated by the Loader at boot; this schema enforces the structural shape.

id.description: Globally unique id — also the MANDATORY namespace prefix for every registered key (§6).

coreVersion.description: Semver RANGE of the Core this plugin is compatible with (§4); the Loader refuses to load a plugin whose range excludes the running Core.

kind.description: Packaging kind: capability (baked in-process), tool (sidecar/client engine), or app (standalone).

secrets.description: Secret field names the operator fills in the tools tab; stored encrypted in kernel state (kernel-redesign §1 Q1).

compose.description: Path (relative to the plugin) to a docker-compose sidecar fragment; tool kind only.

dependencies.description: Plugin ids that MUST boot first (§4). The Loader topologically sorts on these; a cycle is a hard boot failure.

optionalDependencies.description: Used if present, degraded-but-functional if absent.

provides.description: Namespaced DI tokens this plugin registers into the container (e.g. `inventory.StockService`).

consumes.description: Namespaced contracts this plugin resolves via DI. Every entry must be `provide`d by Core or by a declared dependency (Loader-checked).

permissions.description: Permissions this plugin owns, contributed to the global RBAC catalog the auth plugin seeds. A bare string is the key; an object adds RBAC-UI metadata (group + TH/EN labels). Perms are a global vocabulary (roles bind to bare keys like `user.manage`), so they are NOT id-prefixed — unlike `provides` DI tokens.

rationale.description: Human-readable why this permission is requested, shown at the install confirm (marketplace.md W4).

routes.description: Route prefixes this plugin owns — namespaced by id, e.g. `/crm/*`.

events.description: Event names this plugin emits / listens to (Event Bus, §5).

schema.description: Path to this plugin's config JSON Schema.

migrations.description: Path to this plugin's migrations; run in dependency order (§4).

description.description: One-line human description shown in the Plugins UI.

icon.description: Path (relative to the plugin) to an icon image shown in the Plugins UI.

screenshots.description: Paths (relative to the plugin) to screenshot images shown in the Plugins UI.
